title: Dear Mrs. Gerard — graduation-group switch ghostwritten as mom
html:
TL;DR

  • A single-paragraph email ghostwritten by Yahir in his mother's voice ("My name is Iracema Vargas. I am the mother of Yahir Gonzalez...") to fix a graduation-group scheduling mistake he made.
  • Signed in mom's name; explains he had asked Mrs. Gerard to switch him from group 4 to group 2, but actually grandparents are coming for the 7:30 (group 4) slot. Asks to switch back.
  • Documents the mom's name: Iracema Vargas — first explicit mention in any apple-note ingested so far. Surname Vargas (not Gonzalez) is meaningful for yahir family genealogy.
  • Specimen of Yahir's longstanding pattern of writing letters/emails on behalf of family members. Same shape as the dad-on-DoorDash maneuvering in 2020 11 15 scholarship essays and the FBA-help-for-mom in 2019 11 13 coding zeal.
  • He's the family's literate proxy from a young age.

Key quotes

"My name is Iracema Vargas. I am the mother of Yahir Gonzalez."

"I always to personally apologize for this mistake on my son's behalf"

"He was wrong, and I have set up my parents to come for the 7:30 time."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Iracema Vargas — needs an entity page (mom). She's referenced obliquely throughout but this is her named appearance. Also crystallizes the yahir family pattern of Yahir as cross-language / cross-system intermediary.
  • The grandparents-from-Mexico graduation arrangement is a fact-anchor for family stories.
  • This is a tiny note but it's high-signal about Yahir's identity-as-family-translator.

Open questions

  • "Mrs. Gerard" — Grants Pass High admin? Identifiable?
  • Did the swap go through?

Wikilinks

yahir family · iracema vargas · grants pass or · grants pass teen era
